Description: Year-over-year top-line revenue increase
Why important?: The ultimate measure of whether the business is healthy and growing

Description: New customers minus churned customers in period
Why important?: Shows whether the customer base is expanding or contracting -- a leading signal for future revenue
How do/should we measure it?: needs to be automated with CRM
Initiatives: One customer per month per TM- Wave creation

Description: % of customers lost in a given period
Why important?: Indicates customer satisfaction; competitive pressure; and revenue at risk
How do/should we measure it?: Region specific manual tracking. Need to automate the manual tracking, TM needs to analyse data
Initiatives: Qtlry Sales incentive program, monthly incentive to franchisee,

Description: Total revenue expected from a customer over their entire relationship
Why important?: Prioritizes retention over acquisition; justifies CX investment
How do/should we measure it?: CLT currently available in ZNCL, measurement to be setup via CRM

Description: Revenue per customer minus cost-to-serve
Why important?: Reveals which segments are profitable; guides resource allocation
How do/should we measure it?: Highlighting Negative Margin Customer, Low Margin Customer

Description: Supergas share of industrial LPG in served regions
Why important?: Contextualizes growth against the addressable market; important for strategic planning
How do/should we measure it?: Challenges in getting the market share

Description: % of cold contacts that agree to a first discovery meeting or a technical site audit
Why important?: Measures marketing and sales effectiveness at the very top of the industrial sales funnel
How do/should we measure it?:
AS-IS: Manual Excel / Maadhyam 
To Be: will automated in CRM
Initiatives: One customer per month per TM- Wave creation

Description: % of qualified leads that become signed customers
Why important?: Each qualified lead represents significant sales investment; low close rate means wasted pipeline effort
How do/should we measure it?: will be automated in CRM
Initiatives: One customer per month per TM- Wave creation

Description: Post-proposal survey question: "Did the Supergas team provide clear, unbiased information regarding the cost-benefit analysis of switching your fuel source?"
Why important?: Switching fuel sources is a high-risk decision for industrial buyers; perceived trust directly determines willingness to convert
How do/should we measure it?: Feedback survey to be added through CRM

Description: Post-proposal survey question: "How effectively did our engineering team address your specific manufacturing energy needs and transition concerns?"
Why important?: The engineering team's ability to address specific energy needs is the core value proposition in industrial LPG
How do/should we measure it?: Feedback survey to be added through CRM

Description: Total business days from the moment an industrial lead is qualified to the moment the formal contract is signed
Why important?: Industrial buyers compare multiple suppliers; speed from qualification to signed contract is a competitive differentiator
How do/should we measure it?: will be automated through CRM
Initiatives: One customer per month per TM- Wave creation

Description: Total cold reachouts by SHV by channel
Why important?: Start of funnel, leads directly to new customers
How do/should we measure it?: will be automated through CRM
Initiatives: One customer per month per TM- Wave creation

Description: Total incoming inquiries by source
Why important?: Volume signal; low volume means pipeline is drying up
How do/should we measure it?: Manual Excel / Maadhyam
Initiatives: One customer per month per TM- Wave creation

Description: Total count of unique unmapped interactions that reach out but leave without an interaction
Why important?: Shows possible leads that we are missing
How do/should we measure it?:
Industrial: Manual Excel 
Retail: No
To be: should automate in CRM via CTA link (phone call)
Initiatives: One customer per month per TM- Wave creation

Description: Total of inquiries from industrial clients that come from an incorrect form or phone number
Why important?: Shows clarity of contact channels for industrial type customers
How do/should we measure it?:
Industrial: Manual Excel 
Retail: No
To be: should automate in CRM via CTA link (phone call)
Initiatives: One customer per month per TM- Wave creation

Description: % of qualified leads by original source
Why important?: Reveals which channels produce quality leads not just volume
How do/should we measure it?: Manual Excel / Maadhyam
Initiatives: One customer per month per TM- Wave creation

Description: Total business days from the moment an industrial lead is qualified to the moment the formal technical-commercial proposal is sent
Why important?: Industrial buyers comparing suppliers will go with whoever moves fastest; response time is a competitive differentiator
How do/should we measure it?: will be automated in CRM
Initiatives: One customer per month per TM- Wave creation

Description: The number of times a commercial proposal or Gas Supply Agreement (GSA) goes back and forth due to client objections
Why important?: Frequent revision cycles signal misalignment between what sales promises and what operations can deliver
How do/should we measure it?: Automation - CRM, Monthly review required

Description: The net days from the first industrial site inspection to the delivery of the engineering feasibility report
Why important?: The engineering report is a critical trust milestone; slow turnaround undermines perceived technical competence
How do/should we measure it?: CRM- Pre sale deliverable tracker

Description: How many days it takes for the prospective client to grant your engineers physical access to their plant for the initial energy audit
Why important?: Separates customer-caused delay from internal delay -- necessary to measure your own sales velocity accurately
How do/should we measure it?: CRM - Pre sale deliverable tracker

Description: % of prospects who start but don't complete the application/contract process
Why important?: Flags friction in paperwork; KYC; or compliance steps
How do/should we measure it?: Manual tracking, this will get covered in CRM
Initiatives: One customer per month per TM- Wave creation

Description: Number of contract iterations needed to get to the final contactper client
Why important?: Knowing if the current template is understandable
How do/should we measure it?: CLM to overcome manual tracking

Description: % of signed contracts that reach first successful delivery (Customer commissioning)
Why important?: Measures the onboarding handoff; drop-off here means wasted acquisition spend
How do/should we measure it?: Manual tracking by Sales team, Automation from CRM as well

Description: How satisfied were you with the updates and transparency provided by Supergas regarding your installation timeline?
Why important?: Happy onboarding predicts retention; being left in the dark during installation erodes trust before the relationship starts
How do/should we measure it?: In CRM, commucation to the customer on the task list with timeline is fixed

Description: Asked right after the first delivery: "How easy or difficult was it to coordinate the site preparation and installation with the Supergas engineering team?"
Why important?: High effort during onboarding sets a negative baseline for the entire relationship; measures whether the handoff from sales to operations is smooth for the customer
How do/should we measure it?: Currently manul tracking documented; in CRM this is automatedq

Description: The number of days a project is stalled because the client failed to provide site access, complete civil foundations, or clear local statutory permissions
Why important?: Distinguishes client-caused stalls from Supergas-caused delays; identifies where proactive client enablement support is needed (relevant for new-built projects
How do/should we measure it?: Project WIP tracker, manuel excel. Will be developed in CRM

Description: Days from contract signed to equipment installed
Why important?: Customers want to start using product; long waits erode goodwill
How do/should we measure it?: Project WIP tracker, manuel excel. Will be developed in CRM

Description: The percentage of gas system installations that pass the final internal safety check and statutory inspections on the very first attempt without requiring engineering re-work
Why important?: Installation rework is expensive and delays the customer's first use of LPG; high first-pass rate signals engineering quality

Description: Post training survey question: "How satisfied are you with the training provided?"
Why important?: Measuring the quality of the training
How do/should we measure it?: Currently feedback taken from participants post the training

Description: % of first deliveries that arrive on promised date
Why important?: Reliability signal at the very first service interaction
How do/should we measure it?: For every order, on time delivery to be tracked; presently data is available in powerbi

Description: Annual/biannual 'How likely are you to recommend Supergas?'
Why important?: Established benchmark; measures overall brand health and loyalty
How do/should we measure it?: Currently done through external vendor; needs to uploaded in CRM platform

Description: Periodic pulse check: "Rate your satisfaction with the safety compliance, professionalism, and scheduling predictability of our delivery team."
Why important?: The delivery team is the most frequent face-to-face touchpoint; their professionalism shapes the ongoing relationship more than any other interaction
How do/should we measure it?: Currently CSAT rasied after every order, but customer updates optionally

Description: "When there is a delivery delay, price change, or issue affecting your LPG supply, how satisfied are you with Supergas proactively informing you before you had to ask?"
Why important?: Being surprised by a supply problem is far worse than being informed ahead of time -- this is a critical trust driver in industrial supply relationships
How do/should we measure it?: Pro active communication on Price change, Supply issues during crisis period in place. CES feedback is taken twice a year on communication parameter. To be stored in CRM

Description: "How confident are you that Supergas equipment and bulk LPG installations on your site meet the highest safety standards?"
Why important?: Safety is the number one concern for industrial LPG buyers; perceived safety risk trumps price and service quality in retention decisions
How do/should we measure it?: Needs to be captured via SG Care App after evert safety audit

Description: Amount of customers that run out of gas without placing an order on time
Why important?: Help reduce the amount of customers that run out of gas
How do/should we measure it?: Needs further checking for 2026 data

Description: When Supergas conducts mandatory safety audits at the client's plant, how quickly (or slowly) does the client act on the safety recommendations provided?
Why important?: Slow rectification of safety findings signals either customer disengagement or a deteriorating relationship
How do/should we measure it?: Create the scope of work, tracking through SG Care App

Description: Frequency in which Supergas has to do an audit to customers
How do/should we measure it?: Tracking is being done through SG Care App

Description: Amount of issues during the audit sorted out by type of issue
How do/should we measure it?: Tech - Track through SG Care, Sales - powerApp

Description: Average number of orders per customer per month/quarter
Why important?: Declining frequency signals dissatisfaction or competitive encroachment
How do/should we measure it?: SG Care App; CA takes report from SG Care and SM reviews with team
Influences: Vol, margin, business KPIs, customer creation

Description: Number of cylinders ordered per customer
How do/should we measure it?: CTR;
Influences: Vol, margin, business KPIs, customer creation

Description: Amount of orders created in each channel
Why important?: Identify when people avoid the app can help with the digital adoption

Description: Amount of orders left incomplete in the app or other channels
Why important?: Understand when the app or other systems are having failures
How do/should we measure it?: Should be monitored through SG care

Description: Amount of product customers have at all moments
Why important?: Make sure customers are not left out of gas

Description: Number of orders placed when they are about to run out of gas
Why important?: Prevent last-minute orders
How do/should we measure it?: Urgent order registered by Customer service tracked by Sales team

Description: "How easy or difficult is it to place, modify, and track your LPG orders with Supergas?"
Why important?: Ordering friction is a hidden churn driver; if placing orders is hard, customers will seek alternatives with simpler processes
How do/should we measure it?: Ordering  currently not added in CES feedback; To be added in CES for monitoring

Description: How often a client cancels or shifts a scheduled tanker delivery within 24-48 hours of the delivery window
Why important?: Frequent last-minute cancellations create operational waste and may indicate the customer is sourcing from competitors
How do/should we measure it?: Sales reviews with OPS- Filled return analysis

Description: % of deliveries arriving within promised window
Why important?: Core operational reliability; directly drives customer trust and satisfaction
How do/should we measure it?: on-time delivery if it happens within 2hrs from the promised time

Description: % of deliveries that generate customer feedback
Why important?: Low feedback rate means you're flying blind on daily experience
How do/should we measure it?: CSAT currenlty available only on orders and complaints

Description: Number of complaints categorized by issue type (late delivery; product quality; billing; etc.)
Why important?: Identifies systematic problems; rising complaints in one category demands immediate attention
How do/should we measure it?: Categorization as per complaint raised in SG care

Description: Amount of time that it takes customers to receive the credit note for the unfinished cylinders they returned
Why important?: The client gets back the correct amount of credit on time to avoid frustrations

Description: Customer satisfaction with invoicing clarity; accuracy; and process
Why important?: Billing frustration is a leading cause of churn in B2B; often overlooked

Description: A brief quarterly question sent specifically to the client's Accounting/Finance team (not the plant manager): "How easy or difficult does Supergas make it to reconcile and pay your energy invoices?"
Why important?: The client's finance team has a separate experience from the plant manager; billing friction can kill a contract even when operations are satisfied

Description: Customer satisfaction with how billing disputes are resolved
Why important?: Billing disputes are high-emotion moments; poor handling accelerates churn

Description: Revenue lost to billing errors; unbilled services; incorrect rates; or disputes
Why important?: Direct margin loss; every % of leakage is pure profit lost

Description: This tracks how many hours/days it takes to issue a credit note or correct an invoice error
Why important?: Slow resolution keeps the customer in a state of frustration
How do/should we measure it?: we have TAT defined for vraious AR activities - CN / DN - 3 days max
Notes: CN are issued on Monthly Basis against pre-agreed discount (generally at the end of the Month) however CN related to Price difference / errors in Billing etc are issued during the month within 3 Days once it is approved by ZSH, Gas Residual / Filled / Defective returns related CN are issued by Plant on daily basis post their inspection and review

Description: % of invoices with no errors (wrong amount; wrong product; wrong customer)
Why important?: Errors trigger disputes; slow payment; and erode trust
Notes: Invoicing cycle is automatically done in SAP on the basis of Customer master / Price / Tax conditon / Material etc updated in SAP, these fields are updated by ERP team hence no end -user can modify it, any error in billing can only be a result of wrong updation in SAP.

Description: The percentage of clients who utilize an online customer portal to view, verify, and pay their bills versus those who require manual paperwork, phone call reminders, and physical checks
Why important?: Self-serve customers have lower cost-to-serve, fewer billing disputes, and faster payment; low adoption means high manual overhead
Notes: Many Big Corporate Houses like - Asian Paints etc want that Invoice copy should be direclty uploaded in their System for processing & payment

Description: % of invoices not paid within terms
Why important?: Cash flow risk; high delinquency suggests customer dissatisfaction or affordability issues
How do/should we measure it?: Various KPI's are in place to measure the AR performance like DSO , CEI , % Overdue etc

Description: Customer satisfaction with maintenance and support interactions
Why important?: Measures whether support is solving problems effectively
How do/should we measure it?: Need to tracked through CRM

Description: % of complaints resolved within SLA timeframe
Why important?: Core service quality; slow resolution erodes trust faster than the original problem
How do/should we measure it?: Tracking with stipulated TAT of 4 days

Description: 'How likely are you to recommend Supergas after your issue was resolved?'
Why important?: The moment of truth -- a well-handled complaint can increase loyalty more than no complaint at all
How do/should we measure it?: CSAT complaint closure feedback available

Description: % of customers who log more than one complaint in a period
Why important?: Indicates unresolved systemic issues; repeat complaints mean the fix didn't work
How do/should we measure it?: A3 problem solving - RCA

Description: Number of tickets categorized (delivery issue; equipment failure; billing; emergency)
Why important?: Identifies problem hotspots; rising volume in a category demands root cause investigation

Description: Qualitative sentiment analysis of customer feedback by issue category
Why important?: Reveals which issues are emotionally charged vs. transactional
How do/should we measure it?: Needs to be through CRM

Description: Time from ticket created to first human response
Why important?: Speed of acknowledgment; sets the tone for the support experience
How do/should we measure it?: Responding back as per the guidleines

Description: % of tickets resolved on first interaction without escalation or follow-up
Why important?: Efficiency and effectiveness in one metric; high FCR = lower cost and happier customers
How do/should we measure it?: Overall complaint tracking ongoing;but FCR tracked

KPI: First-Time Fix Rate (FTFR) for Technical Faults
Description: This tracks the % of technical issues resolved during the very first maintenance visit
Why important?: Equipment downtime stops the customer's production; fixing on the first visit minimizes production loss and demonstrates technical competence

Description: % of churned customers who return within X months
Why important?: Some churn is recoverable; measuring this tells you whether win-back efforts work
How do/should we measure it?: Manual tracking through MIS; CRM tracking going forward

Description: NPS collected from customers at point of departure
Why important?: The most honest feedback you'll ever get; reveals real reasons for leaving
How do/should we measure it?: Need to be added through CRM

Description: Days from churn notification to final account closure and settlement
Why important?: Efficient offboarding matters; drawn-out closure leaves a bad last impression and ties up resources
How do/should we measure it?: CCR Report
Notes: Accounts Clousre / F&F Settlement generally depends upon Negotiation and recovery from customer which sometime takes longer time than expected, Successful termination cases are reported in Customer Contract Report (CCR) on Monthly basis

Description: Number of exits categorized
Why important?: Identification of most common reasons for clients to exit
How do/should we measure it?: Currently manual tracking, added in CRM

Description: Composite of leading indicators: order decline >20%; payment delays >30 days; complaint spike
Why important?: Early warning system; allows intervention before the customer formally leaves
How do/should we measure it?: Red Flag Review, No Sales for 3 Months , Declining in Collection, Non Adherance to Payment Terms etc

Description: % of churning customers who complete the exit survey
Why important?: Low completion = blind churn; you don't know why they left
How do/should we measure it?: To be added in CRM

Description: Days from contract end to equipment collection
Why important?: Uncollected equipment is an asset sitting idle; delays suggest process gaps
How do/should we measure it?: Equipement collection (cylinder) within 7-10 days;
